atk.name: Ace

His 2 speed / 2 armor balance makes him a solid front-liner — not a pure anchor, but responsive enough to adapt to roamer threats. Ace plays best as a dedicated hard breacher who pre-plans breach vectors and coordinates team timing around S.E.L.M.A. deployment. His ability to breach walls, hatches
strengths: Throws 3 S.E.L.M.A. charges per round — redundancy and flexibility

Recommend Ace for stacks with coordinated roamer denial (Nomad, Gridlock) and support operators. The nerfed speed makes Ace less forgiving for casual play—positioning mistakes are more punishing. Consider recommending Thermite or Buck as primary hard breachers instead, reserving Ace for specific maps where remote S.E.L.M.A. deployment has clear advantages. atk.name: Thermite

Charges cannot be countered or destroyed during the burn—once placed and activated, the breach is guaranteed and permanent. This makes him the most reliable hard breacher in the game.

atk.playstyle: Thermite is the archetypal hard breacher and foundational operator for virtually every coordinated attack. His role is straightforward: place charges on reinforced walls, protect those charges during the burn, and establish control of the breached area. He is not an entry fragger but rather a setup operator who enables the entire team's site execution. 🟠 Orange — A camera is currently being watched
🔴 Red — Enemies are nearby
5 charges total; charges drain slowly while the Radar is active
Snake cannot use his Primary Weapon while the Radar is active — this is the core tactical tradeoff
tips: Activate Radar at the start of your floor push to read anchor positions before committing
Switch to Precision Mode to identify facing direction — don't peek into a defender who's already watching your angle
Drop the Radar before taking gunfights — you can't shoot your primary while it's up
Coordinate with Lion: Lion scans all floors simultaneously, Snake's Radar provides floor-level precision including facing angles and objective location
Scavenge breach charges or frags from fallen teammates mid-round — OSP extends utility into late rounds when gadgets are depleted

atk.playstyle: Role: Intel/Front Line attacker. Best used to read defenders before a push. High adaptability with OSP in late rounds. Strengths: - Floor-level precision intel including defender facing direction (Precision Mode) — better tactical detail than Lion's whole-building scan - Cannot be countered the same way as traditional drones — it's held by Snake, not deployed into the building - OSP turns a depleted late-round into a resourced one — Breach Charges and frags scavenged from dead teammates are huge
atk.stackNotes: - Slug — Was best candidate to pilot Solid Snake due to Lion + Valkyrie intel-layering identity. *(Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21 — reassess for current main stack. Peej runs Ash/Brava ATK and Maestro/Valkyrie DEF; Grant remains the primary intel-stack candidate.)* - Grant — Now primary candidate (Slug on B Team). Already running Lion as Y11S1 ATK lead. Stacking Soliton + Lion on the same team is the highest value pairing. Grant is the primary Snake pilot candidate going forward. - OSP is a bonus, not a reason to pick Snake — no one on this stack should draft Snake purely for gadget scavenging.

atk.name: Striker

gadget.name: Gadget Kit (Flexible Secondary Gadget System)
gadget.description: Striker has a unique "Gadget Kit" that allows her to select any 2 types of attacker secondary gadgets to bring into the field. One gadget occupies the unique gadget slot, providing flexibility to adapt to team needs. Striker can bring duplicate gadgets (e.g., 2x Frag Grenades) or mix different utilities (e.g., Breach Charge + Claymore) based on team composition.
mechanics: Choose from ANY 2 types of attacker secondary gadgets (Frag Grenades, Hard Breach Charges, Smoke Grenades, Stun Grenades, Breach Charges, Impact EMP Grenades, Claymores)
One gadget type occupies the unique gadget slot
Maximum flexibility—adapt to team needs before each round
Carries the quantities of each gadget type selected (e.g., 2x Frag, 3x Breach = 5 total utility items)
Hard Breach Charges + Impact EMP Grenades → Self-sufficient hard breacher
Breach Charges + Claymores → Flank-watch vertical plays
Stun Grenades + Frag Grenades → Entry fragger unit
Smoke Grenades + Stuns/Claymores → Safe pushes and plant support
tips: Assess team composition before gadget selection—identify utility gaps
Communicate gadget choice with team to avoid redundancy
Stack multiple gadgets if team needs heavy utility (3 Breach Charges for wall breaking)
Use flexibility to adapt to unexpected roamer aggression or site adjustments
Consider balancing utility—don't overspecialize on one gadget type
Share extra utility with teammates if they run out mid-round

coachingRec.primary:
Option A: M4 + Magnified/ACOG scope
Option B: SR25 (DMR)
coachingRec.secondary gadget: Flexible—flashbangs + frags, frags + claymores, or hard breach + frags
coachingRec.rationale: M4 with magnified scope for consistent crosshair advantage at all ranges; FH for vertical recoil control. SR25 DMR follows full DMR standard with MB + HG and no laser since DMRs involve holding angles. Always secondary shotgun over pistol when available.

atk.playstyle: Striker is a flexible support operator with balanced 2-2 stats and exceptional gadget adaptability. Her playstyle centers on team utility—she selects gadgets to fill team composition gaps and enable synergistic plays. Striker is played as a support flex operator, adapting her gadget setup round-to-round based on team needs and site layout. Striker excels in coordinated teams with specialized roles that may lack certain utilities (e.g., a team heavy on gunfighters but light on gadget denial). Her
strengths: Gadget Kit enables extreme adaptability—adjust gadgets each round
Fills utility gaps in team composition without sacrificing gunplay
Can specialize in heavy utility stacking (3 Breach Charges, 3 Stun Grenades)
Balanced 2-2 stats provide durability and mobility
Flexible primary weapon options (M4, M249, SR-25)
Zero skill floor on gadget—pure utility selection
Enables team compositions that would otherwise lack key utilities
weaknesses: No unique gadget mechanic—less impactful than specialized operators
Gadget effectiveness depends entirely on team coordination
Poor gadget selection cripples team utility coverage
Cannot replace dedicated hard breachers or intel operators if team lacks them
Purely support-focused—low individual impact potential
Requires team communication and planning
Can be out-utility'd by coordinated enemy teams
atk.stackNotes: Striker is a solid pick for coordinated stack play where gadget selection is discussed pre-round. She requires no mechanical skill but demands team communication. Recommend for players who enjoy support roles and understand team utility needs. Works well in casual stacks with flexible team compositions. Emphasize pre-round gadget selection planning and avoiding redundancy with team gadgets. Good teaching operator for new players learning team utility concepts without complex mechanics. Not recommended for players who prefer specialists with defined roles (breachers, intel, duelists).

def.name: Bandit

gadget.name: Shock Wire (CED-1)
gadget.description: Crude Electrical Device (CED-1) — Shock Wires are electronic devices that attach to metallic gadgets and electrify them. Each Shock Wire takes 2 seconds to deploy. Bandit carries 4 Shock Wires per round.
mechanics: Deploy on reinforced walls, barbed wire, deployable shields, or castle panels
One Shock Wire attaches to ONE metallic gadget; adjacent gadgets do not get electrified regardless of proximity
Same gadget can be shocked by multiple Shock Wires, but stacking does not increase effect
Shock Wires are electronic gadgets (can be detected/destroyed by IQ, Thatcher, Twitch, etc.)
Visually identifiable by small electric arcs emanating from the electrified surface
Electrified reinforced walls destroy placed breaching charges:
Thermite's Exothermic Charges — destroyed before detonation
Hibana's X-KAIROS pellets — destroyed before detonation

coachingRec.rationale: MP7 has no horizontal recoil so FH; laser because constantly placing bandit batteries and C4, then immediately needing ADS; Keratos if you want to open wall holes (high-caliber rotation tool), P12 if you won't use it for that; C4 essential.

def.playstyle: Bandit is a wall-control specialist who denies hard-breach attempts through wall electrification. His role is to prevent Thermite, Hibana, and Ace from breaching critical reinforced walls, forcing attackers to adapt their breach strategy or expend additional utility to clear Shock Wires first. Bandit operates at two distinct skill tiers: Tier 1 (Beginner): Deploy Shock Wires on predicted breach locations during setup phase and let them persist. No timing required. Moderate value—attackers can co
strengths: Unique breach destruction mechanism — only operator who destroys placed charges before detonation
"Bandit trick" potential — elite mechanic allows skilled players to make walls completely unkillable
Flexible gadget usage — Shock Wires on walls for breach denial, on barbed wire for damage and slowing
High mobility (3 speed) enables roaming between wall locations and flanking after wall defense
Minimal setup requirement — doesn't require wall setup beyond standard reinforcement
Team synergy potential — pairs with Castle for electrified panel denial (Y10S4 synergy)
Established meta pick — consistently high pick rate across skill levels due to wall control value
weaknesses: Skill-dependent value — Bandit tricking requires precise timing; beginner players get minimal value from advanced mechanic
Extremely fragile (1 armor) — loses direct gunfights; must rely on positioning, timing, and team support
Twitch hard counter — Twitch drone can destroy multiple Shock Wires from range, nullifying entire wall defense
IQ reveals positions — IQ electronics scanner highlights Shock Wire locations, enabling attacker pre-planning
Limited resource — 4 total Shock Wires is restrictive on multi-wall breach maps
Requires wall setup — if walls are already destroyed or not reinforced, Bandit has no targets
Predictable positioning — experienced attackers know Bandit positions near walls and can pre-aim/pre-fire
def.stackNotes: - Bandit is a core pick for any reinforced-wall-heavy site defense strategy - Primary use case: Pair Bandit with Castle (Y10S4 synergy) on key entries for unkillable denial - Alternative synergy: Bandit + Mute on highly breach-heavy maps (Bank, Consulate, Coastline) for redundant utility denial - For developing Bandit players: Begin with setup-phase Shock Wire placement (beginner strategy—low skill, moderate value), then practice reactive timing against custom game AI - Progression path: Move from setup placement → reactive timing → "Bandit trick" execution as game sense develops - Listen for

def.name: Castle

gadget.name: UTP1 Armor Panels
gadget.description: Universal Tactical Panels (UTP1) — Castle deploys up to 4 bulletproof barricades that can be placed on doorways and windows. These panels are significantly more durable than standard wooden barricades and are constructed from carbon nanotubes bonded with polymer chains, providing exceptional strength at low weight.
mechanics: Deploy up to 4 Armor Panels per round
Can be placed on any doorway or window frame (replaces standard barricade)
Panels are bulletproof and cannot be penetrated by gunfire (except BOSG.12.2 which requires 9 shots = 3 layers)
Requires 9 melee attacks to destroy (3 layers, 3 hits per layer); reduced from 12 in Y6S3.3
Panels protect Castle while being deployed but only the portions being rolled down
Panels take increased time for defenders to pull off (regenerates another panel if defender pulls off)
Panels placed on doorways have small gap at bottom (like reinforcements) allowing drones/projectiles underneath
Bulletproof from gunfire — withstands sustained fire and precision damage

coachingRec.muzzle: EB (or Comp if horizontal recoil issue)
coachingRec.secondary gadget: BP Camera (for info on one key angle) or Proxy alarms (info on multiple angles)—site-dependent
coachingRec.rationale: UMP45 has almost no recoil so EB gives free DPS. HG because with low recoil there's no benefit to VG. Laser because horizontal grip usually means more aggressive play. BP cam vs proxy depends on whether you want concentrated or distributed info.

def.playstyle: Castle is a site anchor and reinforcement specialist focused on controlling attacker entry routes and forcing utility expenditure. His role is to delay attackers through durable barrier placement, maximizing time for roamers to reposition or for defenders to prepare for mid-round engagement. Castle places his 4 panels on the most critical entry routes during setup phase—typically the main attacker push vectors. His panels force attackers to either spend hard-breach utility (Thermite, Hibana, Ace
strengths: Delayed entry advantage — panels force attackers to spend time or utility on breaching
Y10S4 electrification synergy — paired with Bandit/Kaid, panels become nearly unbreachable (electrified reinforced-wall-equivalent)
Vision/sound denial — opaque panels deny attacker information and audio cues from site
4 flexible placements — can cover multiple entry routes or stack for redundant denial on critical entrance
Durable under fire — 2 armor allows maintaining position while attackers breach
Team synergy with Bandit/Kaid — creates unkillable site entrance control
weaknesses: Utility destruction — hard-breach operators (Thermite, Hibana, Ace) destroy panels without melee; panels are not breach-resistant like reinforced walls
Limited coverage — 4 panels cannot defend all entry routes on maps with multiple breaches
Explosive counters — Breach Charges, Ash breaching rounds, Zofia Impact Grenades destroy instantly
Slow movement (2 speed) limits rotation flexibility if site setup fails
Requires setup coordination — optimal value requires paired Electroclaw operator (Bandit/Kaid); standalone Castle has moderate value
Countered by: Thermite/Hibana/Ace (hard breach), Sledge (melee hammer), Maverick (torch burn-through), Caveira (silent melee + interrogation near panels)
def.stackNotes: - Castle is a core pick for any controlled-entry site defense strategy with paired Electroclaw operator (Bandit or Kaid) - Primary use case: Place panels on 2–3 critical entry routes, coordinate with Bandit/Kaid for electrification - Y10S4 electrification synergy change: Pair Castle with Kaid (has wider range than Bandit) for maximum coverage - Panel placement strategy: prioritize main attacker entry routes; save panels for critical chokepoints, not every doorway - Bulletproof Camera provides intelligence gathering from panel positions; use to coordinate rotations - Against hard-breach heavy c

def.name: Kaid

gadget.name: "Rtila" Electroclaw (×2)
gadget.description: Kaid deploys two throwable electrified claws that attach to surfaces and electrify metal gadgets within a 1.4-meter radius. Each claw provides 4-second charging time before activating. Electroclaws electrify reinforced walls, hatches, barbed wire, deployable shields, and Castle armor panels—creating area denial without requiring physical placement directly on the gadget.
mechanics: Throw up to 2 electrified claws per round
Claws attach to various surfaces automatically
After attachment, 4-second charge-up period before electricity activates
Visual radius indicator displays during charge-up showing electrification range
1.4-meter effective radius per claw (can electrify 3-width reinforced walls if centered)
Radius penetrates walls—electrifies metal objects on opposite side of wall if radius reaches them
Reinforced walls
Hatches and hatch covers

coachingRec.rationale: Never run AUG — terrible gun; TCSG12 with ACOG + suppressor + VG + laser; suppressor for positional concealment on a gun that holds long angles; do not run the high-caliber "Han Solo" pistol secondary — run the standard pistol with laser; C4 if available.

def.playstyle: Kaid is a heavy-armored passive anchor who specializes in area denial through electrical barriers. Unlike Bandit (active battery management), Kaid throws electrified claws once and provides long-term area denial without requiring repositioning. His role is to force attackers into resource-intensive breach attempts or route changes. Kaid's strength lies in denying vertical plays (hatches) and protecting multiple reinforced surfaces simultaneously from distance. His critical Y10S4 synergy with Cas
strengths: Versatile Electrification: Covers walls, hatches, barbed wire, shields, and Castle panels
Throwable Design: Greater range and flexibility than Bandit; hatch denial without hatch reinforcement
Castle Synergy: Electrified Castle panels are near-impassable; forces attackers into heavy utility investment
Heavy Armor: 3-armor enables aggressive anchor positioning and trade durability
Passive After Placement: Claws work autonomously; frees Kaid to hold angles without active gadget management
Area Denial: Radius penetrates walls, electrifying opposite-side objects; dense coverage per claw
weaknesses: Slow Repositioning: 1-speed limits rotations and redeployment after initial placement
Limited Claws: Only 2 claws vs. Bandit's 3 batteries; less total coverage
Slow Charge Time: 4-second activation delay allows attackers to destroy claws pre-activation
Hard Counters: Thatcher EMP, IQ scanner, Twitch drone, Kali lance, Zero ARGUS, Flores RCE-RATERO

Positional Commitment: Must commit to early placement; repositioning wastes claws
def.stackNotes: Grant's primary anchor pick. Throw electrified claws onto reinforced walls early—prioritize key breach points. CRITICAL: Coordinate with Castle to electrify his armor panels (Y10S4 game-changer). This combo forces attackers into extremely resource-heavy breaches, making it meta-defining. Hold site anchor with heavy armor; use AUG A3 for sustained fire. Place claws reactively to deny specific attacker routes based on callouts. Strong on sites with multiple reinforced walls and hatches (Bank, Coastline, Chalet). Pair with Rook for armored stack or Doc for medical support. Don't reposition claws

def.name: Mute

Large spherical area of effect (estimated 7-9 meter radius)
Remain active for entire round unless destroyed
All jammers operate simultaneously; effects do not stack but provide overlapping coverage
Blocks all attacker drones from operating/passing through jammer radius
Drones cannot enter jammed zone; forced to stay outside or be disabled
Applies to all reconnaissance drones: Twitch Shock Drones, Valkyrie Black Eyes, IQ cameras, etc.

Position jammers at objective site to deny attacker drones late-round
Use jammers to deny specific gadgets: place one to block likely Dokkaebi call positions
Protect jammers: hold them secret so attackers waste utility destroying them
Coordinate with Bandit/Kaid: wall jamming + wall electrification = unkillable walls
Early-round jammers deny initial recon if placed in drone pathways

coachingRec.rationale: Identical primary setup to Smoke but with C4 instead of barb wire. C4 on Mute specifically because it's a powerful gadget and Mute's jammers already handle the area-denial role that barb wire would fill.

def.playstyle: Mute is a heavy anchor specializing in electronic denial and breach prevention. His core function is denying attacker electronic gadgets through jammer placement. Unlike roamers, Mute remains site-proximal to protect his jammers and respond to destruction attempts. He excels on reinforced-wall-heavy sites where jammer placement near breach points provides maximum value. Mute's playstyle centers on early jammer setup near walls or objective site, followed by defensive anchoring. During setup phas
strengths: Breach Prevention: Jammers block Thermite, Hibana, and Ace charge detonation — direct counter to hard-breachers
Drone Denial: Multiple jammers create extensive no-fly zones for attacker reconnaissance
Flexible Coverage: Works against multiple attacker gadget types (Dokkaebi, Goyo, IQ, Gridlock, etc.)
Persistent Gadget: 4 jammers remain active all round; early placement provides all-game value
Wall Synergy: Jammers + Bandit electrification = walls become unkillable (denial + destruction prevention)
3-Armor Durability: Heavy armor allows holding defensive positions and protecting jammer areas
4 Placements: Four jammers allow coverage of multiple site areas or layered denial zones
weaknesses: Twitch Vulnerability: Twitch drones can systematically destroy all 4 jammers from range if unprotected
EMP Vulnerability: Thatcher's EMP disables jammers for 10 seconds (soft counter, not permanent)
Jammer Fragility: Small profile; once located, attackers can destroy with minimal utility
Placement Skill Floor: Optimal jammer placement requires deep map knowledge
Pre-Placement Requirement: Jammers must be set up before attackers arrive; aggressive early breach can bypass
Slow Movement: 1-speed limits repositioning and rotation
IQ Scanner Counter: IQ's Electronics Detector reveals all jammer locations through walls
def.stackNotes: Grant leads the stack on Mute — 114r / 54.4% / 1.32 K/D in Y11S1, his highest DEF volume by a wide margin. For stack composition: Mute is a strong pick on sites with multiple wall breaches (e.g., Bank vault, Coastline). Pair him with Bandit or Kaid if wall control is the primary defensive strategy. On objective-centric defenses, Mute provides strong early-round value. New stack players learning wall defense should consider Mute after mastering Bandit, as his gadget provides more forgiving placement compared to Bandit's real-time shock wire management. Place jammers near predicted breach points

def.name: Tubarao

coachingRec.grip: Option A: HG / Option B: HG

def.playstyle: Tubarão is a balanced anti-entry operator designed to disrupt attacker utility timing through temporary gadget freezing. His playstyle combines gadget denial with area control and vertical tracking capabilities. Unlike traditional utility denial (destruction), Tubarão freezes gadgets for 12 seconds, forcing attackers to wait out the duration or destroy the canister. This creates timing pressure and decision-making tension. His 2-2 stats enable flexible positioning while maintaining gadget covera
strengths: Unique 12-second gadget deactivation mechanic (not destruction)
Footprint tracking reveals vertical attacker movement
Multiple canisters create overlapping denial windows
Excellent against synchronized breacher attacks
Disrupts attacker timing without destroying utility
Balanced 2-2 stats enable both area denial and fragging
Synergizes well with gadget denial operators and trap operators
weaknesses: Maverick Tactical Breaching Torch melts ice (direct counter)
Gadget deactivation is temporary (12 seconds only)
Explosions (grenades, Zofia KS-79) can destroy canisters
Freezing affects friendly gadgets equally (no discrimination)
Limited value if attackers wait out deactivation duration
Attackers can destroy canisters before deploying utility
Ying can disable goggles but becomes vulnerable to her own Candela
def.stackNotes: Tubarão is an excellent anti-breach operator for disrupting Thermite, Hibana, and Ace attacks. Place Zoto canisters on common breach points and entry pathways. Use footprint tracking to identify vertical attacker movement. Hold flexible positions with 2-2 stats. Coordinate canister placement with team to deny specific utility. Stack canisters for overlapping denial windows. Strong against coordinated breacher attacks. Pairs well with gadget denial operators (Bandit, Kaid, Jäger) and trap operators (Kapkan, Thorn, Fenrir). ---

def.name: Caveira

gadget.name: Silent Step + Interrogation
gadget.description: **Gadget Setup:** Caveira's gadget is unique because it combines two integrated mechanics: **(1) Silent Step (Passive):** Caveira moves silently with zero audio signature at any movement speed (walking, running, sprinting). Attackers cannot hear her footsteps or position through audio cues. **(2) Interrogation (Active):** When Caveira interacts with a downed (DBNO) attacker, she interrogates them, forcing a confession that reveals the real-time position of ALL living attackers to her entire team for ~10 seconds.

coachingRec.rationale: Run shotgun as primary — Luison already covers range with almost zero recoil; no laser on Luison because Cav is sneaky and laser reveals position; shotgun iron sights + laser for faster ADS when forced into CQB; impacts for dynamic rotates and map movement.

def.playstyle: Caveira is an elite roaming operator who specializes in eliminating isolated attackers through ambush and extracting team-wide intelligence through interrogation. Her role is to hunt attackers in secondary/flanking positions, down them (usually through ambush advantage), and interrogate for complete attacker positioning. Caveira's playstyle is aggressive roaming focused on isolation. During early-round, she uses Silent Step to position herself on ambush routes before attackers arrive. When attac
strengths: Unique interrogation mechanic — only operator who reveals ALL enemy positions simultaneously; unmatched intel value
Silent movement advantage — Silent Step allows positioning without audio warning; ambush advantage is significant
Roaming flexibility (3 speed) — rapid repositioning across map; can hunt multiple attacker routes
Team intel multiplier — single interrogation provides entire team information; indirect value is enormous
Suppressed weapon — Luison is quiet; silent executions don't alert distant attackers to Caveira's position
DBNO guarantee on Luison — ensures downed targets for interrogation even with headshots
weaknesses: Extremely fragile (1 armor) — loses direct gunfights against prepared attackers; depends on ambush advantage
Requires downed targets — cannot interrogate already-dead attackers; must specifically down targets (not kill)
Interrogation interruptible — teammate revive or Caveira death stops interrogation execution
Team dependent — if teammates don't follow up on interrogation intel, value is wasted
Vulnerable while executing — during interrogation animation, Caveira is stationary and vulnerable to fire
Predictable after interrogation — after interrogating, Caveira's position often revealed through team reactions
Countered by: Dokkaebi (Logic Bomb calls interrupt execution voice line, though interrogation still triggers), Jackal (if Silent Step deactivated), attacker coordination preventing isolation
def.stackNotes: - Caveira is a core roaming pick for stacks with communication and follow-up capability - Optimal use case: roam independently on map flanks; isolate one attacker early-round for interrogation - Upon successful interrogation, call out entire attacker team positioning to enable coordinated team response - Pair with other roamers (Vigil, Ella, Kapkan) that benefit from interrogation intel - When teammates cannot capitalize on intel (site anchors too committed), skip interrogation and reposition for next target - Luison silent headshots = guaranteed DBNO for interrogation setup - Against coordina

def.name: Clash

gadget.description: Crowd Control Electro Shield (CCE Shield) — an extendable electrified riot shield that serves as Clash's primary weapon. When deployed, the shield covers her entire front body and generates a high-voltage Charged Field Generator (CFG) that emanates directly in front of the shield in a cylindrical shape. mechanics: Full-body front protection when deployed
Extends to cover entire front-facing side
Cannot be carried like normal ballistic shields
Opaque barrier prevents visual contact through shield
Deployable on ground and remotely activated for cover
Only deployed form, not handheld carry option

def.playstyle: Clash is a unique defender designed to create temporary area denial and frustration through active blocking rather than passive traps. Her electrified riot shield forces attackers to either spend significant utility to eliminate her, find alternative routes, or commit to a dangerous melee assault. She plays as a roamer deterrent and entry blocker—her presence in a corridor slows and damages aggressive pushes, buying time for teammates to rotate and set up defensive positions. Her primary strengt
strengths: Full-body protection — provides bulletproof cover and blocks line of sight
Charged Field Generator damage/slow — continuous damage to attackers in front of shield; slows aggressive pushes
Forces utility expenditure — attackers must use grenades, C4, or flashbangs to neutralize her

weaknesses: Limited offensive capability — cannot fire primary weapon while shield is active; switch to secondary for damage

def.name: Ela

Her 3-speed/1-armor profile enables rapid repositioning and aggressive roaming playstyle. Ela's strategy unfolds in two phases: (1) Setup Phase: Place 1-2 mines on
strengths: Action Phase Flexibility: Unlike traditional trappers, Ela can deploy mines reactively during combat — not limited to setup-phase placement

Higher skill floor than Vigil or Kapkan. For stack application: Ela should be tested as aggressive roamer alongside Caveira, Vigil, Kapkan, and Lesion. Her action-phase placement and concussion combo create high-skill ceiling gameplay. Recommend Ela picks by mechanically skilled players (Grant, Smigs, Sarge) who can consistently convert concussion advantage into eliminations. For roaming composition: Pair Ela with Cave

weaknesses: Noise Creation: Dashing creates audible alert; eliminates surprise if misused

Not ideal for static anchor positions—roam aggressively and leverage vertical control.

During early-round when drone activity is high, his ERC-7 provides maximum value. As ro

strengths: Extreme counter to Ying, Blitz, and all flash-heavy attacks

def.name: Echo

def.name: Sentry

Coordinate gas timing with team: deploy gas when attackers are committed to plant/defuse. Pair with Maestro for coordinated denial (Maestro identifies plant, Smoke denies it). def.stackNotes: Hound is the stack's primary Frost player — 20r / 55.0% / 1.07 K/D in Y11S1, outperforming his Castle line (42r / 50.0% / 0.89). The data supports a roaming/trapping preference over pure anchor play. For stack application: Frost should be core roaming option alongside Caveira, Vigil, Ela, Kapkan, and Lesion. Hound's success suggests stack values instant incapacitation mechanics for coordinated execution plays. Place traps on high-traffic entry points (prioritize windows for vault mechanics). Position Frost or nearby roamer to execute immediately upon trap activation. For roaming composition: P

def.name: Kapkan

gadget.name: EDD MK II Entry Denial Device (×5)
gadget.description: Kapkan deploys five laser tripwire mines on doorways and windows. Each mine creates an invisible horizontal laser beam across the entry point. When any attacker crosses the laser, the mine detonates for 60 damage within 3-meter radius. Mines must be placed on barricadable entry frames; they trigger on attacker contact only (friendlies cannot trigger, drones pass through).
mechanics: 5 EDD mines per round; attach to doorway/window frames
Invisible horizontal laser tripwire across entry
Multiple mines can stack on same frame
Placement height varies: windows chest-height minimum, head-height maximum; doorways ankle-to-head height
Laser triggers when ANY attacker touches it
60 damage per detonation; 3-meter effective blast radius (drops off harshly)
No friendly fire—defenders cannot trigger mines
Drones pass through unaffected

coachingRec.secondary gadget: Bulletproof Camera or Barbed Wire (BP Cam for deep roam intel; Barb for approach slowing into trap zones)
coachingRec.rationale: FH for VSN vertical recoil; VG for high-volume AR; laser because Kapkan constantly places/stows gadgets before fighting.

def.playstyle: Kapkan is a balanced roaming trapper who specializes in early-round damage through laser tripwire mines. Unlike passive trappers, Kapkan places mines for area denial, then actively roams to capitalize on weakened/distracted attackers. His 2-speed/2-armor enables both trap management and direct fragging. Kapkan's strategy: Place mines on forced entry routes during setup. Listen for detonations to locate attackers. Roam to secondary positions to finish weakened targets or ambush distracted units c
strengths: Free Early Damage: Mines damage attackers immediately upon entry—soft damage before gunfights
5 Mine Coverage: More mines than comparable trappers; layerable on key entries
Roaming Flexibility: Balanced stats enable holding or roaming; not locked into anchor
Experienced Player Performance: Grant (stack player) achieves 2.07 K/D with Kapkan—proven elite-level effectiveness
weaknesses: Visible When Deployed: Mines visible unless perfectly hidden in environmental obstruction
Skill Dependent: Beginner placement is ineffective; experienced teams destroy mines instantly
Limited Damage: 60 damage doesn't guarantee kills; value is softening, not finishing
Drones Counter Mines: Attacker drones scout and mark mine positions for easy clearing
def.stackNotes: Grant is elite Kapkan player: 2.07 K/D. Place mines on forced entry routes. Listen for detonations to locate attackers. Roam for cleanup picks. Core roaming operator alongside Caveira, Vigil, Ela. --- ---

def.name: Lesion

gadget.name: Gu Mine (×8)
gadget.description: Lesion deploys eight invisible poison mines that are placed at floor level. When an attacker steps on a mine, it lodges in their body, dealing ~5 HP/second poison damage while revealing their health status to Lesion on a gadget display. Attackers can remove mines by spending ~5 seconds pulling them out (unable to move during removal). Multiple mines can lodge in same attacker, stacking damage and debuffs.
mechanics: 8 Gu mines per round; placed at floor level on any surface
Invisible to attackers; detection requires close inspection
Triggers when any attacker steps on it
Mine lodges in attacker's body upon contact
Damage Rate: ~5 HP/second (approx. 60 HP per 12-second engagement)
Duration: Persists until attacker removes mine or dies
Health Tracking: Lesion sees poisoned attacker's health status on gadget display; rough location revealed
Multiple Stacks: Multiple mines on same attacker stack damage and debuffs

coachingRec.rationale: Laser essential — Lesion swings off goo mines when enemy is hit; need instant ADS on swing; VG standard; FH for vertical recoil; BP Cam over observation blockers (observation blockers useless per video).

def.playstyle: Lesion is a balanced roaming trapper specializing in attrition and information gathering. Unlike instant-incapacitation trappers (Frost) or burst-damage trappers (Kapkan), Lesion provides gradual poison damage and health intel. His 8 mines allow extensive coverage; his 2-speed/2-armor enables flexible roaming. Lesion places mines on roam paths during setup, then rotates to secondary positions. Poisoned attackers are weakened gradually while Lesion gains health information via gadget display. He
strengths: Persistent Attrition: Poison damage continues throughout engagement; weakens even without direct follow-up
Health Intelligence: Lesion sees poisoned attacker health; provides intel without drone/camera
8 Mine Capacity: Most mines of any trapper; enables extensive area coverage
Invisible & Sticky: Difficult to spot if well-placed; surprise damage advantage
Stacking Potential: Multiple mines create severe health drain and debuff
Roaming Flexibility: 2-speed enables roaming after placement; hunt weakened targets
weaknesses: Gradual Damage: Poison is slow; doesn't immediately threaten unlike burst trappers
Mine Removal: Attackers can remove mines; spending 5 seconds creates 5-second window for team engagement
Moderate Durability: 2-speed/2-armor is moderate; direct gunfight vulnerability if caught alone
Utility Vulnerability: Grenades and utilities destroy multiple mines if focused on mine locations
Delayed Value: Unlike Kapkan (instant) or Frost (incapacitation), Lesion's value is conditional and delayed
def.stackNotes: Lesion is a depth trapper-roamer in the stack — no player runs him as a primary pick (unlike Kapkan, which has strong tracked data across multiple players). Situational use on maps with dense roam corridors where attrition pressure is high-value. Place 8 mines on roam paths and entries. Pair with Caveira (poison-weakened attackers easier to interrogate) or Kapkan (burst + poison attrition). Call out poisoned attacker health to roaming teammates. Effective on maps with many roam corridors (Bank, Chalet, Kafe). ---

def.name: Melusi

gadget.name: Banshee Sonic Defence
gadget.description: Four Banshee Sonic Defence devices — deployable, bulletproof sound emitters generating standing waves (18-19 Hz) that produce disorienting effects and movement slowdown on nearby attackers. Each Banshee is stationary, autonomous (requires no manual control), and creates a dual-layer effect: audio alert + speed reduction.
mechanics: 4 Banshee devices per round; deployable on walls or floors
Devices are bulletproof when inactive; can only be destroyed by explosives or melee attacks
Gunfire does not damage closed Banshees
Banshees operate autonomously once placed—no user control required
Activates when attackers enter radius AND have line of sight to device
Activation emits loud buzzing noise alerting all defenders
Effect applies even to prone attackers
Applies even through drone holes and small soft wall destruction gaps
tips: Place Banshees in high-traffic corridors and common roam routes for early-round intel
Stack multiple Banshees in narrow choke points for maximum slowdown (effects stack)
Position Banshees at door/window entrances to slow aggressive pusher entry
Use audio alerts to call out attacker positions to team
Banshees act as radar—activation tells defenders attackers are pushing
Pair Banshees with traps (Frost mats, Kapkan traps) to catch slowed attackers

coachingRec.rationale: EB for low-recoil MP5 DPS boost; laser for swing-off-Banshee play (enemy slowed → swing and shoot); VG because MP5 still needs recoil help; BP Cam stacks with Melusi's gadget info value — maximize intel generation.

def.playstyle: Melusi is a heavy anchor specializing in area denial and crowd-control through sonic disruption. Unlike roamers or aggressive operators, she functions as a defensive anchor who uses passive Banshee placement to create denial zones. Her 3-armor and slow movement speed position her as a site-oriented defender rather than a mobile responder. Melusi's value centers on information gathering (audio alerts when Banshees activate) and forcing attackers into controlled entry paths by making roaming space
strengths: Persistent Area Denial: Four Banshees create extensive denial zones without resource consumption
Dual Effect: Audio alert + movement slowdown provides both intel and active pressure
Stacking Slowdown: Multiple overlapping Banshees create near-total movement lockdown on narrow choke points
Autonomous Operation: Once placed, Banshees require no manual control—Melusi can play normally
Heavy Armor: 3-armor profile allows holding defensive positions under fire
Trap Synergy: Slowed attackers are vulnerable to Frost, Kapkan, tachanka traps
Information Tool: Banshee activation tells team attackers are pushing specific routes
weaknesses: Slow Movement Speed: 1-speed limits repositioning and rotation; vulnerable if caught away from site
Predictable Placement: Experienced attackers expect Banshee placement in corridors
Explosive Vulnerability: Attackers can destroy Banshees with grenades or utility
Finka Counter: Finka's Adrenal Surge bypasses slowdown (Banshee still alerts)
Brava Hack: Kludge Drone hacks Banshee, turning it against defenders
Does Not Stop Pushes: Slowdown deters but does not prevent determined attacker entry
Limited Active Contribution: Banshees are passive; Melusi provides limited direct combat pressure
def.stackNotes: Melusi is a strong defensive anchor for denying roam space and protecting site from aggressive entry. Place Banshees in common roaming corridors early in the round. Use audio alerts to call out attacker positions. Hold site anchor positions rather than roaming aggressively. Coordinate with teammates to exploit slowed attackers when Banshees activate. Not ideal for aggressive rotation—play anchored near site. Pairs well with other denial operators (Frost, Kapkan, Tachanka) and armor providers (Rook, Doc).

def.name: Alibi

gadget.name: Prisma
gadget.description: Prisma — a pair of holographic decoys that project volumetric displays of Alibi. Each decoy is a three-dimensional illusion created using laser light suspended dust particles, displayed at over 1600 dpi resolution. When activated, a Prisma displays a static image of Alibi in default uniform holding an unmodified Mx4 Storm in standing position.
mechanics: Deploy up to 3 Prismas during the round
Prismas activate when thrown on the floor with sufficient space (minimum floor clearance required)
Activation takes 1 second — Prisma unfolds and projects volumetric display
If insufficient space, Prisma enters "jammed" state with blinking red light and fails to deploy
Prismas are destroyed if a barricade is placed on a doorway where they sit
Display shows Alibi in standing, idle position
Model faces the same direction as the thrower when deployed
Display is completely static (no animation or movement)

coachingRec.rationale: ACS12 functions as a DMR — ACOG access, no movement penalty, large magazine, horizontal grip for lateral follow-up recoil; laser because playing more aggressive than anchor; always secondary shotgun (Bailiff) over Keratos; proxies for info vs. observation blockers (useless).

def.playstyle: Alibi is a high-speed roamer designed to gather intelligence and disrupt attacker confidence through deception. Her Prisma decoys work best when placed early in the round to establish baseline intel on attacker approaches and rotations. She is NOT an anchor—she roams the map using her decoys as intelligence tools to ping inquisitive attackers and coordinate team responses. The Prisma gadget functions as both a psychological deterrent and an information-gathering tool. Attackers hesitate to push
strengths: Strong information gathering through decoy pings visible to all defenders
High speed (3/1 profile) enables aggressive roaming and map pressure
Decoys create psychological pressure and waste attacker time and utility
Low-risk intel tool—decoys cost nothing if not triggered
Excellent at identifying attacker positions early; pings show exact location vector
Decoy identity concealment forces attackers to assume all defenders are hidden
weaknesses: Extremely fragile (1 armor) in direct firefights
Decoys provide no area denial or utility destruction
Skilled attackers quickly learn to ignore decoys after early identification
Vulnerable to information-heavy attacks (IQ detects Prismas, Dokkaebi calls reveal positions)
Glaz — Thermal Scope does not detect volumetric displays; can bypass Prisma cover
Jackal — Eyenox footprints reveal defender positions, bypassing Alibi's identity concealment
Nøkk — Does not trigger Prisma tracking when gadget active (Hereford cloak prevents ping)
def.stackNotes: - Strong pick for aggressive roaming stacks that value early-round information gathering - Place 1-2 decoys early (first 30 seconds) in high-traffic chokepoints to establish baseline intel on attacker rotations - Roam independently after decoy placement; use pings to call out positions for teammates - Pairs well with other roamers (Vigil, Ela, Caveira) for multi-angle catches - NOT a primary anchor—anchor sites with Rook, Tachanka, or Doc instead - Nitro Cell alternative allows denial of rotates; evaluate site setup and roam range before choosing gadget - In post-plant, use Prisma pings to tra

def.name: Azami

gadget.name: Kiba Barrier
gadget.description: Kiba Barrier — a set of kunai-style throwing spikes that embed into surfaces and deploy expandable foam barriers. When thrown, each spike releases hardening foam that creates a bulletproof barrier. Barriers can block off entrances, patch breach holes, create defensive angles, and provide cover.
mechanics: Throw kunai-style Kiba spikes at any surface (walls, floors, ceilings, doors, hatches)
Spikes embed into the surface on impact
Foam releases from spike and expands, then hardens into solid bulletproof barrier
Barriers persist for entire round unless destroyed
Can be placed on unreinforced walls, reinforced walls, hatches, and destructible surfaces
Multiple barriers can be stacked on same wall or surface
Spikes require aiming and positioning due to arc trajectory
Deployment saves essential time compared to traditional reinforcement

coachingRec.primary: ACS12 (as DMR config: ACOG + HG + laser) OR 9mm C1 (Holo + FH + VG + laser) — dual option based on intended range
coachingRec.secondary: Deagle (muzzle + laser) — same on both configs
coachingRec.rationale: ACS12 = play at range, cover from distance (DMR standard: ACOG + HG). 9mm C1 = close-range Kiba Barrier anchoring. Impacts create new hard surfaces mid-round for dynamic Kiba Barrier deployment. Choose config before round based on site and intended playstyle.

def.playstyle: Azami is a balanced defender (2/2 profile) designed to provide flexible reinforcement and create unexpected defensive angles through barrier placement. Her playstyle combines dynamic site adaptation with active defensive engagement, allowing mid-round reinforcement changes based on attacker utility and breach patterns. Unlike traditional reinforcement (walls/hatches/barricades), Azami's barriers allow real-time adaptation to attacker behavior. If attackers breach or destroy sections of site cove
strengths: Flexible reinforcement adapts to attacker utility and breaches
Creates unexpected defensive angles and peek windows
Abundant spike usage allows dynamic mid-round reinforcement throughout round
Can patch bullet holes and breach gaps without full reinforcement
Allows vertical control through floor and ceiling barriers
Balanced stats (2/2) enable both angle-holding and active gadget usage
Quick deployment saves time compared to traditional reinforcement
weaknesses: Barriers are destructible by sustained gunfire—not as durable as reinforced walls
Requires precise aiming and positioning; skill-dependent tool
Barriers destroyed by melee (3 hits) and specific explosives (grenades, impact charges)
Sledge's Breaching Hammer destroys barrier in one hit—direct hard counter
Ash's Breaching Rounds destroy barriers
Kali's LV Lances destroy barriers
Zofia's Impact Grenades destroy barriers
def.stackNotes: - Strong flexible defender for sites requiring rapid adaptation to attacker utility and breaches - Use Kiba spikes to create dynamic reinforcement based on attacker patterns—don't pre-plan all barriers - Patch bullet holes in reinforced walls to maintain defensive integrity after attacker fire - Create peek windows on key site positions to establish unexpected angles - Coordinate barrier placement with team to establish overlapping multi-angle defense - Adapt site setup reactively based on drone information—place barriers where attackers will breach - Hold site positions with 2/2 stats; balanc

def.name: Denari

gadget.name: T.R.I.P Connector
gadget.description: T.R.I.P Connector (Tactical Responsive Interconnected Prism) — a deployable area-denial device that creates laser tripwire grids. Denari carries 7 T.R.I.P Connectors per round. When at least two T.R.I.P Connectors are deployed within each other's line of sight, they generate a laser beam connecting them. Any attacker passing through the laser is damaged and slowed.
mechanics: Deploy up to 7 T.R.I.P Connectors per round
Stick to almost any surface (walls, floors, ceilings, doors, etc.)
Devices are unprotected and can be destroyed with firearms or explosives
Preview system shows laser grid connections before deploying
Displays which potential laser connections each device will form
Defenders can pass through lasers safely with no effect
Teammates of Denari unaffected by lasers
Requires minimum 2 T.R.I.P Connectors in line of sight

coachingRec.rationale: FMG9 best primary option; laser for swing-off-laser-wire ADS; shotgun secondary for opening rotates — iron sights + HG because no PvP use, just for opening walls, so no need for standard secondary shotgun rule; deployable shield over observation blockers (observation blockers useless).

def.playstyle: Denari is a light, aggressive roaming operator who specializes in creating laser-grid area-denial networks to disrupt attacker positioning and routes. His role is to place T.R.I.P Connectors strategically on common attacker paths, forcing them to either destroy devices, find alternative routes, or suffer damage and slowness from laser activation. Unlike traditional area-denial operators (Lesion, Melusi), Denari provides visible, predictable denial that requires attacker reaction and utility expe
strengths: Visible area denial — laser grids create psychological pressure and force attacker decision-making
7 deployable devices — extensive coverage allows multi-zone laser networks across site
Damage + slow effect — attackers passing through lasers are damaged and movement-restricted
Flexible placement — works on any surface (walls, floors, ceilings) for creative denial
Preview system — see laser connections before deploying, enabling strategic planning
Light armor (3 speed) — rapid repositioning and aggressive roaming capability
weaknesses: Visible and vulnerable — lasers are highly visible; attackers easily locate and destroy devices
Exposed devices — no protection mechanism; guns, explosives, drones destroy with no mitigation
Attacker utility destruction — Thatcher EMP, Twitch drones, Brava Kludge, Zero Argus easily neutralize devices
Predictable placement — experienced attackers anticipate common laser positions and routes
Slow to deploy full network — 7 devices require time to place; setup-dependent value
Reliant on device survival — if destroyed mid-round, defender loses area denial
Electronic gadget: susceptible to Thatcher EMP disabling
def.stackNotes: - Denari is a specialized roaming area-denial pick for early-round laser network setup - Optimal placement: common attacker pathways and entry routes during setup phase - Use T.R.I.P preview system to identify high-value laser connections before deploying - Prioritize placing 2-3 connector pairs that overlap for magnified denial effect - During setup, place most devices; mid-round placement becomes reactive only - Communicate device locations to team so teammates understand denial zones - Against Thatcher-heavy comps, save device placement for after EMP window passes - Pair with Lesion/Frost f

def.name: Doc

gadget.name: MPD-0 Stim Pistol
gadget.description: MPD-0 Stim Pistol — a pressurized CO2-powered hypodermic delivery system that fires epinephrine/saline stim shots at targets. Doc carries the Stim Pistol with 3 total charges per round that regenerate (verified charge regeneration in-game). Each shot heals targets to 100 HP and can overheal beyond max health to 140 HP (temporary health buffer). Downed teammates can be revived remotely with a Stim shot at approximately 145 HP.
mechanics: Charge count: 3 shots per round (regenerates over time during round)
Healing effect: each shot heals target to 100 HP (full health)
Overheal capacity: shots heal beyond 100 HP maximum up to 140 HP temporary health
Overheal decay: temporary health decays at 1 HP per second once overheal obtained
Overheal maximum: even with multiple stims, maximum overheal is 140 HP (140 extra temporary health); cannot exceed this cap
Lesion interaction: Doc stim removes Gu Needle poison from Lesion-affected attackers
Revive shot: downed (DBNO) teammates revived at 145 HP via single stim shot
Revive time: 2.75 seconds (faster than manual revive's 4 seconds)

coachingRec.rationale: EB for DPS boost on 27-damage MP5; VG because without it recoil becomes unmanageable; laser because without ACOG Doc plays more aggressive; always secondary shotgun (Bailiff) over pistols; BP Cam or barb wire depends on team's existing info coverage.

def.playstyle: Doc is an anchor support operator who extends defender survivability through healing and revive capability. His role is to keep teammates alive, healthy, and fighting—rather than inflicting damage himself. Doc is typically positioned at or near objective site, where he can see and support multiple teammates. His healing allows defenders to hold positions longer, survive additional shots, and give downed teammates a second chance to fight. Doc's playstyle revolves around maintaining high team hea
strengths: Unique revive mechanic — only operator with remote revive capability; can bring downed teammates back into fight without risk
Overheal value — 140 HP overheal provides additional health pool; effectively increases team durability permanently
Sustained anchor power — healing allows single defenders to hold positions longer against multi-attacker pressure
Flexible weapon options — three primary weapon options allow Doc to adapt to site layout (shotgun for tight areas, SMGs for open)
Armor resilience — 3 armor allows Doc to take poke damage and stay alive while supporting teammates
Team multiplier effect — healing one teammate enables that teammate to win gunfights; indirect value is enormous
weaknesses: Dependent on teammate positioning — Doc's value is proportional to team grouping; if teammates are spread out, he cannot heal efficiently
Limited charges — 3 shots per round with regeneration means Doc cannot continuously heal; must be strategic with usage
Vulnerable while healing — line-of-sight requirement means Doc is exposed when healing teammates
Counterable revives — attackers can attempt to execute downed teammates before Doc revives them (though revive time is fast)
Healing denial — if Doc is killed or disabled (Dokkaebi call), healing stops and team loses support
Countered by: Attackers with sustained damage output, IQ (gadget scanner reveals Doc positions), Gridlock (limits positioning)
def.stackNotes: - Doc is a core anchor support pick for site-focused defenses with Rook - Optimal placement: anchor position with sight lines to multiple teammates (vault, basement, etc.) - Position for visibility first — defensive value scales with number of teammates you can heal - Prioritize overhealing teammates before they take damage (preventative healing > reactive healing) - Save revive shots for critical moments (downed anchor during post-plant, downed primary entry-holder mid-round) - Pair Doc with Rook for maximum team durability: Rook armor + Doc overheal = nearly unkillable anchors - Use Bulletpr

def.name: Jager

gadget.name: ADS-MKIV "Magpie" (×3)
gadget.description: Jäger deploys three Active Defense Systems that automatically intercept and destroy incoming projectiles before they detonate. The ADS is a miniaturized military-grade defense system that detects and fires at grenades, flashbangs, smoke grenades, impact grenades, and other attacker utility within a 5.5-meter radius.
mechanics: Jäger deploys 3 ADS units per round
ADS can be mounted on walls, floors, and other interactive surfaces
Each ADS is a mounted, stationary device (cannot be moved mid-round once placed)
ADS electronic gadget — susceptible to electronic countermeasures
Effective Range: 5.5 meters from ADS center
Line of Sight Required: ADS only intercepts projectiles in direct line of sight
ADS maintains a steady blue glow indicating active status
Projectiles must enter both range AND line of sight to be intercepted

coachingRec.rationale: 417 is one of the best DEF guns; significant horizontal recoil — Comp over FH; always P10C over P12 — P10C has integrated sight making it far easier to shoot; laser; BP Cam over observation blockers (observation blockers useless per video).

def.playstyle: Jäger is a light-armored roaming defender who specializes in early-round utility denial through automatic projectile interception. Unlike anchor-focused operators (Doc, Rook, Maestro), Jäger places ADS units for team-wide protection, then actively roams to hunt attackers and leverage his 3-speed advantage. His role bridges utility support and aggressive roaming. Jäger's playstyle unfolds in two phases: (1) Setup Phase: Deploy 3 ADS units on critical zones—objective site for grenade spam protecti
strengths: Unique Automatic Denial: ADS is the only operator gadget that automatically destroys incoming projectiles—no other defender provides this capability
Team-Wide Value: ADS protects entire team from grenades, smoke, flashes, etc.; not just Jäger's personal utility
Passive Operation: Once placed, ADS works autonomously; Jäger can roam without monitoring the system
Flexible Roaming: 3-speed enables rapid repositioning and aggressive flanking after ADS placement
Weapon Flexibility: Shotgun for close-range roaming, 416-C for mid-range engagements
Early-Round Impact: Denies attacker utility-heavy approaches, forcing adaptation to breaching/direct engagement
weaknesses: Fragile Profile: 1-armor/3-speed loses direct gunfights against most attackers when caught alone
Limited Interceptions: ADS can only destroy 1 projectile at a time; grenade spam depletes charges quickly
ADS Destruction Vulnerability: Attackers can target and destroy ADS units with focused fire; poor placement = worthless gadget
Requires Active Roaming: Unlike Doc/Rook (passive value), Jäger's value depends on active fragging; ADS alone insufficient
Hard Counters:
Thatcher's EMP disables ADS temporarily
Twitch Shock Drone destroys ADS from range safely
def.stackNotes: Jäger is a strong utility denial operator for stack integration. Place ADS units on objective site to protect anchors from grenade spam during push phase. Deploy second ADS on roam paths to enable early-round aggression without utility denial fear. Third ADS can be held as backup or placed on secondary roam zone. Position ADS where attackers cannot easily destroy with direct fire. Protect ADS by anchoring nearby or assigning teammates to cover ADS locations. Save ADS by spreading attacker grenades—if they throw 3 grenades, only 2 are destroyed; force utility expenditure. Jäger roams aggressive

def.name: Rook

gadget.name: Armor Pack
gadget.description: Body armor distribution pack — single deployable armor bag containing armor plates granting +20 bonus armor per pickup. All defending teammates can pick up plates individually throughout the round. Armor persists on map until destroyed.
mechanics: One armor bag per round; deployed at round start on objective site
Bag placement is permanent; persists entire round unless destroyed
All defending teammates can individually pickup armor plates
Each plate grants +20 bonus armor (increased durability)
Armor stacks with operator base armor; cumulative effect
Rook himself receives +20 armor from his own bag
Teammates interact with bag to equip armor plates
Plates equip instantly; no animation or delay
tips: Place armor bag centrally on objective where all teammates can easily access
Position bag in safe location not easily accessible to attackers
Call out bag location to team; ensure all teammates know placement
Encourage early-round armor pickup before attackers arrive
Protect bag location from destruction; don't leave it exposed to attacker angles
Place bag before attack phase so all defenders can equip

primaries.notes: Primary weapon; sustained fire for site defense

coachingRec.rationale: No laser — pure anchor, already ADS before fights start; laser reveals position with zero ADS benefit. EB because MP5 recoil is low enough it doesn't need FH/Comp — EB gives free DPS. C4 for anchor breach response.

def.playstyle: Rook is a pure support anchor specializing in team durability and survivability through passive armor distribution. His core value is granting all teammates +20 bonus armor, increasing their effective HP pool. Unlike operators with active gadgets requiring management, Rook's role is simple: place armor bag and play objective site defense normally. Rook's playstyle is straightforward: deploy armor bag on objective during setup phase, then anchor site with MP5 sustained fire. His 3-armor and heavy
strengths: Team Durability Boost: Armor grants entire defending team +20 bonus armor; scales with team size
Passive Value: Gadget requires no active management; deploy and defend normally
Beginner-Friendly: Lowest skill floor operator; simple setup and execution
Persistent Bag: Armor bag remains accessible entire round; supports late spawns and revives
Armor Stacking: Five defenders with armor = cumulative 100+ HP boost for team
Survival Enabler: Extra durability keeps teammates alive longer to execute site defense
No Resource Cost: Infinite armor availability once bag deployed
weaknesses: Passive Gadget: Armor bag provides no active pressure or defensive tools
No Intel: Rook provides no cameras, drones, or information gathering
Bag Destruction Risk: Armor bag can be destroyed if located/focused by attackers
Requires Teammate Discipline: Team must actively pickup armor; coordination needed
No Direct Impact: Gadget enables durability but doesn't stop attacker progress
Slow Movement: 1-speed limits repositioning; vulnerable if caught off-site
Predictable Playstyle: Site-anchoring strategy obvious; limited tactical flexibility
def.stackNotes: Rook is an essential core anchor for any defensive setup. Place armor bag centrally on objective during setup; ensure all teammates can easily access without danger. Call out bag location explicitly to team. Anchor site with MP5 sustained fire; don't roam (defeats armor bag purpose). Pair with other site anchors (Smoke, Maestro) for layered defense. Rook enables developing players to survive longer and learn site defense through durability advantage. Recommended as foundational operator for new stack players. Bag placement is critical: central objective location guarantees team access. Maintai

def.name: Tachanka

gadget.name: Shumikha Launcher
gadget.description: Shumikha Launcher — a grenade launcher firing incendiary thermite shells that detonate on impact and create sustained fire zones on walls, floors, and surfaces.
mechanics: Fires incendiary rounds that ignite on impact and burn for several seconds
Fire can be placed through soft walls or on floors to deny chokepoints
Flames deal damage over time to any attacker walking through
Can shoot through windows, doorways, and soft surfaces for indirect area denial
Unlimited ammo — can continuously deny and reposition fire zones
Fire can be extinguished by Gridlock's Trax Stingers, but this is rarely a concern in ranked
tips: Stack fire at doorframes and stairwells to force attackers to take damage on entry
Shoot through walls to deny plant/defuse positions without exposing yourself
Combine with Smoke's remote canisters — Smoke stops the plant timer while Tachanka flames the approach
Excellent for denying hard breach entries (shoot fire into the hole immediately)
Can peel soft wall reinforcements to create his own fire lines
Place fire on bomb itself to deny defuse (attackers take damage while defusing)

primaries.notes: High damage, large mag, slow ADS — good for suppression through walls

coachingRec.secondary gadget: Barb Wire, BP Cam, or Deployable Shield
coachingRec.rationale: Never run the SMG — why give up half of Tachanka's gadget capability? LMG only; 1x scope is only available scope; Bearing 9 because full-auto pistol always beats semi-auto; secondary gadget is genuinely flexible — barb wire/BP Cam for info and delay, deployable shield as firing hub to deny plant.

def.playstyle: Tachanka is a hard anchor and area denial specialist. Post-rework he shifted from the game's most notorious meme operator (stationary mounted LMG) to a genuinely impactful defender. His strength is in controlling space — forcing attackers to commit resources (grenades, utility) to clear fire zones or take heavy chip damage trying to push through. He plays deep anchor, rarely roaming. His fire zones are most impactful in late-round scenarios where attackers need to plant or defuse under pressure.
strengths: Unlimited area denial that can be repositioned throughout the round
Effective through walls — can deny without line of sight
Exceptional in post-plant scenarios to contest defuse
Great for holding stairs, doorways, and entry points
DP-27 has massive mag size for suppressive fire
weaknesses: 1 speed makes roaming non-viable — pure anchor
Gadget requires him to expose part of his body to aim
Fire can be jumped/vaulted over in some positions
Hard breaches that open large gaps can be difficult to fire into consistently
Low mobility means he's vulnerable to flank roamers clearing site
def.stackNotes: - No current stack player is listed as a primary Tachanka player - Worth considering for Grant or Hound in anchor roles given area denial utility (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21) - The Smoke + Tachanka late-round deny combo is a legitimate strat to develop for Villa and Oregon bomb sites - His 3-armor playstyle fits an anchor-heavy defensive identity ---

def.name: Thunderbird

gadget.name: Kóna Station
gadget.description: Kóna Station ×3 — automated healing dispensers that fire healing pellets to injured or taking-damage operators within 3-meter radius. Stations provide immediate healing (30 HP) plus persistent healing buff, and can revive downed operators within range.
mechanics: Deploy up to 3 stations on floors and surfaces
Takes 3 seconds to fully deploy
Healing radius: exactly 3 meters
Stations automatically heal first injured operator in range OR healthy operator who takes damage
Healing amount: 30 HP per pellet, with excess converted to temporary overheal
After firing healing pellet, station enters 35-second cooldown
Can pick up deployed station and redeploy to reset cooldown faster
Continues healing targeted operator at reduced rate after pellet fired
tips: Place stations on site where team will hold angles
Position to maximize team healing coverage within 3-meter range
Combine with Deployable Shields/ADS/MAG-NET for protected healing zones
Use stations to sustain defenders and reduce medical rotation needs
Enable defenders to hold positions longer without returning to Doc
Stack stations for overlapping coverage on high-traffic areas

coachingRec.rationale: Spear preferred; FH/VG for decent recoil; Thunderbird has no fixed playstyle — gadget is passive so laser depends on whether you play aggressive or hold angles; always secondary shotgun; secondary gadget is genuinely flexible because the healing gadget operates independently of the secondary utility role.

def.playstyle: Thunderbird is a balanced support anchor designed to sustain defensive positions through automatic healing. Her playstyle revolves around strategic station placement to maximize team durability and reduce need for medical rotation. Unlike Doc (who requires activation), Thunderbird's stations provide passive, automatic healing to defenders in range. This allows team members to focus on fragging while sustaining health through attrition. Her 2-2 stats enable her to hold angles effectively while pl
strengths: Automatic passive healing without activation requirements
Multiple stations provide site-wide healing coverage
Heals through obstacles (holes, drone holes) within radius
Enables team self-revive for downed operators in range
Persistent healing buff sustains defenders through engagements
Balanced 2-2 stats allow both fragging and support
Works passively — Thunderbird can engage while stations heal
weaknesses: Stations can be destroyed: explosives, gunfire, melee, laser drones
Limited 3-meter healing radius requires close positioning
Thatcher EMP temporarily disables (doesn't destroy, but denies use)
Brava Kludge Drone hacks stations to self-destruct
Kali LV Lances destroy all stations in range simultaneously
Ram BU-GI can destroy stations out of line-of-sight
35-second cooldown between heals limits burst healing capacity
def.stackNotes: Sarge plays Thunderbird as primary DEF in verified stack data. Place Kóna stations on site in areas where team will hold. Use healing to sustain long site holds and reduce medical rotation needs. Coordinate with armor providers (Rook, Doc) to maximize team durability. Hold site anchor positions with 2-2 stats. Place multiple stations to cover entire site. Strong for enabling aggressive site holds and defensive pushes. Pairs well with other anchors (Tachanka, Aruni, Kaid) and medical support operators (Doc, Rook). ---

def.name: Wamai

gadget.name: Mag-NET System
gadget.description: Mag-NET (Magnetic Neutralizing Electronic Targeting) System — a thrown, adhesive magnetic interceptor that captures incoming attacker projectiles and detonates them harmlessly. Wamai starts with 1 Mag-NET and gains one additional charge every 40 seconds (up to 5 maximum deployments per round). Mag-NETs can stick to any surface except other gadgets.
mechanics: Thrown adhesive devices that stick to surfaces and walls
Active radius: 3.5 meters with line of sight required
When a projectile enters the radius, the Mag-NET stops its flight, resets the detonation timer, attracts it magnetically, and detonates it 3.5 seconds after capture
Each Mag-NET can capture only one projectile at a time (max 6 total across all deployed Mag-NETs)
If a Mag-NET is destroyed before detonation, the captured projectile is dropped and returns to normal behavior
Frag Grenades, Smoke Grenades, Stun Grenades
Impact EMP Grenades, GONNE-6 projectiles
Thatcher's EMP Grenades
tips: Throw Mag-NETs preemptively in high-traffic utility corridors where grenades are likely to be thrown
Stack multiple Mag-NETs in tight chokepoints to create dense projectile denial zones
Use Mag-NET placement to protect roaming areas and flanks, not just the bomb site
Coordinate with Jäger's ADS—understand that ADS takes priority over Mag-NET
Place Mag-NETs in common grenade throw lines and objective approaches
Remember Mag-NETs are fragile; expect attackers to systematically destroy them

coachingRec.rationale: Never AUG — it's terrible; MP5K is low-recoil so EB for DPS (switch to FH only if struggling); laser; always secondary shotgun; impacts — Wamai is active with gadget so dynamic utility fits.

def.playstyle: Wamai is a flexible 2-2 anti-utility defender designed to deny attacker projectiles through electromagnetic capture. His Mag-NET system is unique in that it detonates captured grenades rather than deflecting them (unlike Jäger). He can function as a roaming support or flexible anchor depending on team composition and map positioning. His value lies in forcing attackers to either burn additional utility to clear Mag-NETs, route around denial zones, or commit to direct engagements. By covering hig
strengths: Captures and detonates all thrown projectiles (grenades, flashbangs, drone charges, airjabs, lances, etc.)
Multiple Mag-NETs create extensive and overlapping denial zones
Can place Mag-NETs anywhere (walls, floors, ceilings) for flexible coverage
Balanced 2-2 stats allow flexibility—anchor, roam, or support
Excellent against grenade-heavy attack compositions
Passive gadget allows Wamai to fragging while Mag-NETs work autonomously
Mag-NETs persist for the entire round unless destroyed
weaknesses: Mag-NETs are destructible—attackers can systematically clear them with gunfire
IQ detects Mag-NETs like any electronic gadget
Requires predictive placement—poor positioning wastes charges
Twitch's Shock Drone can destroy Mag-NETs from range
Fuze's Cluster Charges can destroy Mag-NETs while clearing rooms
Kali's LV Lances can destroy Mag-NETs if close enough on deployment
Attackers can learn Mag-NET placement and route around them
def.stackNotes: Wamai is excellent for anti-utility defensive strategies. Place Mag-Nets early in corridors where attackers are likely to throw grenades and utility. Predict expected attacker utility routes based on map knowledge. Use Mag-Net coverage to deny grenade spam and protect key positions. Roam or anchor based on team needs—balanced 2-2 stats are flexible. Pairs well with other utility-denial operators (Jäger, Goyo, Kapkan). Strong pick against grenade-heavy attack lineups. ---
